content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

December 17, 1976

Stephen Reinhardt
Bodie, Fogel, Julber,
Reinhardt and Rothschild
5900 Wilshire Boulevard, Suite 2600
Los Angeles, CA 90036

Re: Opinion Request
No. 76-091

Dear Mr. Reinhardt:

Thank you for your letter of November 19, 1976,
seeking advice with respect to whether Winner/Wagner & Associates, an employer of a lobbyist, can manage the campaign of an
elected state official who will seek state office. I am informed that during telephone conversations with Kenneth Pinney
you decided to seek a formal opinion instead of staff advice.
This letter is to inform you that the Commission will issue an
opinion in response to your request.

The Commission reserves the right to request additional information which the Commission or its staff deems relevant to the preparation of the opinion. A proposed draft opinion will be prepared by the staff for presentation to the Commission and a copy of this draft opinion will be forwarded to
you as soon as it is available.

Pending issuance of the formal opinion, it will be
permissible for the firm of Winner/Wagner & Associates to enter
into a contract to represent the elected state officer, provided
that the firm adheres to two conditions. First, the contract
between Winner/Wagner & Associates and the elected state officer
must be on the same terms as are generally available to other
candidates represented by the firm. In other words, the firm
cannot extend the elected state officer any special discounts or
other extraordinary terms which are not available to other candidates. Second, the firm must refrain from any involvement in
fund raising activities on behalf of the candidate. In particular, the firm cannot engage in any activities which would constitute making a contribution, acting as an agent or intermediary in

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE-LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT-CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or very faint, with only some barely visible marks at the top that are too unclear to transcribe accurately. The rest of the page is white/empty space.

[illegible]

Stephen Reinhardt
Page Two
December 17, 1976


the making of a contribution or arranging for the making of a
contribution to the elected state officer. These limitations
are based on the fact that pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18619(f) Winner/Wagner & Associates is prohibited from using its
assets for any of the activities prohibited by Government Code
Sections 86202, 86203 and 86205.

If you have any questions about this interim advice or
your opinion request, please contact Mr. Finney, Chief of the
Commission's Legal Division.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Michael Bennett
Executive Director

MB:KF:jo

The image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page with only some very faint marks or shadows visible at the top. There is no clearly legible text content to transcribe.
